DOCTORS CENTER AT RED ROCK 7220 S CIMARRON RD STE 155, LAS VEGAS NV 89113-2170
Muscle, Joint, or Bone Problems: N - polyneuropathy
Patient here with a chief complaint of medication refill
**Pt also looking for hand specialist to help with carpal tunnel. Currently seeing neurology and has already
Patient reports diminished activity. She reports voiding urgency (stress incontence). She reports
myalgia and limited motion; fully dependent on caregiver. Hx of frequent falls. She reports skin
lesions (reports improving pressure ulcer on buttom) but reports no itchiness and no skin dryness. She
reports numbness, weakness, tingling, burning, and shooting pain. She reports depression and
anxiety; Worsening anxiety/depression, no SI/HI.. She reports no chest pain and normal heart rate. She
reports no cough, no wheezing, and normal respiration.
Constitutional: Level of Distress: no acute distress. Ambulation: in wheelchair.
Psychiatric: Insight: good judgement. Mental Status: normal mood and affect and active and alert. Orientation:
oriented to person, place, time, time, place, and person.
Lungs: Respiratory effort: no dyspnea. Auscultation: no wheezing, rales/crackles, or rhonchi and breath sounds
normal, good air movement, and CTA except as noted.
Cardiovascular: Heart Auscultation: normal S1 and S2 and RRR and no murmurs.
Musculoskeletal:: Motor Strength and Tone: hypotonicity, abnormal motor strength, and poor tone.
Neurologic: Gait and Station: irregular gait; needs full assistance with ambulation. Sensation:
abnormal. Coordination and Cerebellum: intention tremor.
Patient presented for medication refill. Patient tolerating medication well at current dose without adverse
effects. Refilled as below. Discussed plan with patient, who expressed understanding. Follow up as noted below.
Z76.0: Encounter for issue of repeat prescription
diazepam 2 mg tablet - Take 1 tablet, twice a day for anxiety; F41.9 Qty: (60) tablet Refills: 0
Patient to call and schedule with specialist when able

refills need to be seen in person.

**Complaints of neuropathy, worsening bilateral wrist pains. Gabapentin 800mg increased from BID to TID by
neurology - resent medication. Pt reports improvement
**+3 edema in lower legs. Currently taking 10mg amlodipine - will decrease to 5mg and assess for improvement.
**Pt reports worsening anxiety and depression. Was taking 15mg buspirone BID, but now taking QID. Will add on
diazepam for better control. Will reassess
**Pt has not gotten PT/OT referral - will resend.
**Continues to follow with wound care - improving pressure sore.
